chapters.title: The power of attunement
chapters.text:
Mindfulness is key to attunement. To develop attunement, nurture your ability to remain attentive, and incorporate mindful strategies into memorable social interactions. You should also strive to balance your physical and mental spheres of life. This harmony is an essential factor associated with missing one another, and you can achieve it by:

• Maintaining physical alignment and muscle relaxation.
• Balancing awareness and calmness.
• Striving for self-understanding and understanding others.

It takes consistent effort and practice to learn and develop ways of recognizing emotions and emotion-driven impulses. ~ Edward Brodkin and Ashley Pallathra

As humans, we are created to connect with others from an early age, which is why infants demonstrate a preference for human faces. They develop the ability to grow emotional attachment in a loving environment and get the sense of security necessary to develop themselves and their sense of self. Over time, their minds begin to connect with others through verbal and nonverbal communication.

Actively engage in mindfulness practices to enhance emotional and cognitive development.

As adults, our motivation to have exciting and pleasurable social interactions differs. Some people want to be on their own, while others always seek to be connected to others. But whether introverted or extroverted, we usually strive to create some level of physical, emotional, or intellectual affinity with the people we meet.

All humans have an inherent need for social bonds; if we're deprived of these needs early in life, negative changes may occur in the brain's development and disrupt the ability to cultivate positive attachments to others. This early lack of social interaction with parents or guardians can result in unstable emotions, diminished self-esteem, behavioral problems, and hindered cognitive growth. Social isolation is the worst type of punishment and has adverse effects on a person’s overall health.

chapters.title: The dangers of social isolation
chapters.text:

chapters.title: Social media and authentic connections
chapters.text:
Through social networks, technology makes it easier to maintain relationships. However, the capacity to remain mindfully in tune with others is in jeopardy because the average person is almost always distracted.

Seek out and nurture relationships with people who are empathetic and encouraging.

Remaining mindful during daily interactions is challenging amidst the hustle mentality of modern society. Sometimes, we find it difficult to realize when our attention wanders or when attunement breaks.

Besides social media, thoughts and emotions can also influence how we interact. Even the energy needed to balance your inner and outer worlds can prevent you from being in tune with yourself and those around you.

Quick moments of misattunement often happen, for instance, forgetting a close relative's name. These fleeting moments don't have a lasting impact. Still, they can become problems when we regularly fall out of tune with people, especially someone with the potential to affect our relationships.

During the COVID-19 pandemic, technology provided a great lifeline; it was the only way for patients hospitalized with COVID-19 to see their loved ones. But when we are not amidst a pandemic, this technology can reduce physical human contact and its emotional benefits.

As creatures inherently social and reliant on others for our well-being, we shouldn't let societal influences degrade the quality of our relationships. We must refrain from fostering shallow connections by slowing down and investing in quality interactions with people. Developing attunement skills requires dedication and concentration. The more you integrate it into your everyday routine, the more robust it grows, simplifying and strengthening connections with others.

Did you know? Face-to-face conversations make developing genuine empathy, deeper friendships, love, and productivity easier.

chapters.title: Boost attunement through awareness
chapters.text:
The four components of attunement are :

• Relaxed awareness
• Listening
• Understanding
• Mutual responsiveness

Relaxed awareness is about being conscious of yourself and your surroundings, including others, while maintaining a tranquil state of mind and body. It is the foundation for all other components of attunement.

Cultivate a state of relaxed awareness by first easing your body and then guiding your mind toward calmness.

Although we often associate relaxation with becoming less attentive, developing a balance of awareness with relaxation is possible with practice. Famous award-winning athlete Michael Jordan is an example of someone who achieved this. He is often acutely aware of his movements and that of his teammates and tries to coordinate with them. He is never tense and can remain calm under pressure. The capacity to drive the body to peak physical performance while preserving inner calm is commonly found among athletes.

Relaxed awareness helps you listen, understand, and connect more intimately with your environment and people. This factor is important because you must be aware to be in tune with yourself and another person.

Being aware gives you greater power to engage fully in what is happening. Thus, developing your relaxed awareness is an excellent way to help you self-regulate and maintain balance. For example, try Tai Chi if you're looking for an exercise to increase your awareness while helping you relax.

Did you know? In 2019, the Kenyan long-distance runner Eliud Kipchoge was the first to complete a marathon in under two hours. Despite the pain, he was able to cope and achieve a meditation-like state while remaining aware of the present.

chapters.title: Listen genuinely to build connections
chapters.text:
The ability to listen may seem straightforward, but it isn't. Most people are usually thinking of something else while listening. This partial listening makes it challenging to focus on the whole message that the speaker is communicating through their words and emotions. That is why the moments when we feel others pay attention to us become unforgettable.

Build honest, trusting relationships by paying attention to others' feelings and listening closely.

The first element of genuine listening involves focus. It doesn't just mean using your ears to hear but your attention to absorb every aspect of what is being said, including body language. Listening in this way means avoiding any distraction that fights for your attention.

The second element is synchrony. This approach simply means our facial expressions, body language, and tone of voice begin to mirror the other person as we listen carefully; we do this automatically when we listen.

The third is emotional empathy, which means you can resonate and relate emotionally with another person. You're said to have compassion if you feel emotions similar to those you listen to.

Although listening and emotional empathy involve giving undivided attention to another person, it doesn't demand you lose yourself in the other person's experience.

Looking at someone's face lets the speaker know you're paying attention. While glancing at your phone and talking to someone may feel normal, those small moments add up and can have a terrible impact on a relationship.

As an interviewer, Oprah is an example of a good listener; she’s consistently present and actively avoids distractions.

The simplest form of listening doesn't require words. Imagine walking silently with someone you love, where no conversation is needed, yet you are both acutely connected.

Cultivating this skill can seem challenging for individuals who lacked a parent or caregiver attuned to their needs during childhood. But with some effort, it can be done.

Did you know? Our eyes often offer a wealth of social cues crucial for understanding others.

chapters.title: Put extra effort into understanding others
chapters.text:
The feeling of being genuinely understood after communicating with someone is inexpressible. It feels like the person successfully broke through your barriers, and you want to trust them more.

Let's discuss why this happens and how you can utilize it.

Our brains carry out various processes during interactions to build social cognition.

The components of social cognition include:

• Listening
• Noticing body language and tone of voice
• Trying to understand what is said
• Responding appropriately

It also includes focusing on identifying the emotions expressed on people's faces.

Take part in regular exercises that improve cognitive speed and accuracy.

We are aware of these components sometimes, but they are often unconscious processes our brains carry out without being told. We can help our brains improve at this by intentionally attempting to understand people.

The first step is to recognize emotions. Even though we have been interpreting emotions since birth, this can still feel complex.

The solution is the same old tactic: pay attention to facial expressions and tone of voice. By integrating the visual and auditory information that people display, we convey their emotional state.

Also, avoid being hasty when reacting to emotions and practice self-control and reflectiveness in conversations.

Did you know? At four months, many infants can differentiate between angry or happy faces and prefer looking at happy ones.

chapters.title: The key to genuine attunement
chapters.text:
Mutual responsiveness is the height of attunement. It refers to how we act concerning others and ourselves.

When communicating with others, be initiative and compassionate to train mutual responsiveness.

Practicing mutual responsiveness involves putting others first and meeting them where they are. This approach goes against being fixated on unmet expectations. It also boils down to your willingness to take the initiative in conversations.

This type of engagement opens up communication and increases the likelihood that they will reciprocate the gesture.

However, you must be assertive in practicing mutual responsiveness. Being assertive doesn't mean you have to overpower someone. Rather, it balances your needs, desires, and rights with the other person. Even if you're engaged in an argument and must assert yourself verbally, put your ego aside and avoid shutting others up.

Often, we misunderstand each other, and we do or say things that lead to a lapse in connection for a moment. If these things happen, don't be too hard on yourself; meeting someone halfway is only possible by making humble attempts to understand each other's motives.

Although attunement is personal and deeply rooted in human lives, we are in the age of Artificial Attunement (AA). Today's machines can imitate feelings and sometimes even surpass what people can achieve. The advent of AA has many possibilities; however, human-to-human connections are the best and most valuable. Maintaining interpersonal attunement is essential to our nature and deserves respect and protection despite artificial-technological progress.

Did you know? The origins of artificial attunement trace back to computer science in the 1950s. The field of artificial intelligence (AI) first emerged to develop computers that could simulate aspects of human intelligence.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
While attunement has not received as much attention from researchers as it deserves, its role in our daily lives can't be overstated.

It is clear that attunement is necessary to build long-lasting relationships and help discern the right connections.

The four key components of attunement: relaxed awareness, listening, understanding, and mutual responsiveness work hand-in-hand; with time, you can perfect them. Just remember that practicing relaxed awareness helps you listen more and develop the ability to understand people while being mutually responsive.

It may be difficult for neurotypical people to achieve attunement, but it isn't impossible. The quality of your interactions and your ability to physically communicate and develop relationships determine your growth in attunement.

Exercise such as Tai Chi can also help you develop attunement. Other functional disciplines for learning and practicing attunement include meditation, yoga, music, dance, and stand-up comedy.

Bias and fear stemming from perceived human differences pose a major barrier to attunement and cooperation, yet we can overcome these obstacles.

Social media is another obstacle we should learn to overcome. Spending too much time online reduces your desire to connect in person. And this can have negative consequences for your relationships. To avoid the social media trap, divide your time into designated periods for strictly doing stuff either online or away from your devices.

Try to stick to your schedule — but, of course, be as flexible as possible.

Try this



• Think about a time when you felt a special bond with someone after a great conversation. What was it like?
• Do some mindfulness activities every day to help you stay sharp and attentive.
• Initiate conversations with empathy, striving for depth in social interactions.
• When someone is talking to you, listen carefully and don't let anything distract you.

Read it till the end?
